briefdescription.para: Other interfaces can be added here.
Codec properties that can be set by audio_codec_set_property().
Number of all common channel identifiers.
This and higher values are codec specific.

detaileddescription.para: Refer to the codec's header file.

briefdescription.para: Maximum value describing an audio codec channel identifier.
Audio channel identifiers to use in audio_codec_set_property().

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the codec device
Device errors (bitmask of audio_codec_error_type values)

briefdescription.para: Callback for one frame(block memory) size data transmitted.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the codec device
Pointer to user data

memberdef.argsstring: )(const struct device *dev, uint8_t *buf, uint32_t len, void *user_data)
memberdef.name: audio_codec_rx_done_callback_t

briefdescription.para: Callback for coming one frame size data.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the codec device
Pointer to received data
received data size in bytes
Pointer to user data

detaileddescription.para: Configure the audio codec device according to the configuration parameters provided as input

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for codec driver instance.
Pointer to the structure containing the codec configuration.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, negative error code on failure

briefdescription.para: Set codec to start output audio playback.

detaileddescription.para: Setup the audio codec device to start the audio playback

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for codec driver instance.

briefdescription.para: Set codec to stop output audio playback.

detaileddescription.para: Setup the audio codec device to stop the audio playback

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for codec driver instance.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, audio_property_t property, audio_channel_t channel, audio_property_value_t val)
memberdef.name: audio_codec_set_property

briefdescription.para: Set a codec property defined by audio_property_t.

detaileddescription.para: Set a property such as volume level, clock configuration etc.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for codec driver instance.
The codec property to set
The audio channel for which the property has to be set
pointer to a property value of type audio_codec_property_value_t

simplesect.para: 0 on success, negative error code on failure

detaileddescription.para: Following one or more invocations of audio_codec_set_property, that may have been cached by the driver, audio_codec_apply_properties can be invoked to apply all the properties as atomic as possible

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for codec driver instance.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, negative error code on failure

detaileddescription.para: Clear all codec errors. If an error interrupt exists, it will be de-asserted.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for codec driver instance.

simplesect.para: 0 on success, negative error code on failure

briefdescription.para: Register a callback function for codec error.

detaileddescription.para: The callback will be called from a thread, so I2C or SPI operations are safe. However, the thread's stack is limited and defined by the driver. It is currently up to the caller to ensure that the callback does not overflow the stack.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the audio codec device
The function that should be called when an error is detected fires

simplesect.para: 0 if successful, negative errno code if failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, audio_channel_t channel, uint32_t input)
memberdef.name: audio_codec_route_input

briefdescription.para: Sets up signal routing for a given input channel.

detaileddescription.para: Some codecs can do input routing (multiplexing) from a chosen set of physical inputs. This function maps a given audio (stream) channel to a given physical input terminal.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the audio codec device
The channel to map
The input terminal index, codec-specific

simplesect.para: 0 if successful, negative errno code if failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, audio_channel_t channel, uint32_t output)
memberdef.name: audio_codec_route_output

briefdescription.para: Sets up signal routing for a given output channel.

detaileddescription.para: Some codecs can do output routing (multiplexing) from a chosen set of physical output. This function maps a given audio (stream) channel to a given physical output terminal.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the audio codec device
The channel to map
The output terminal index, codec-specific

simplesect.para: 0 if successful, negative errno code if failure.

briefdescription.para: Set codec to start audio playback or capture.

detaileddescription.para: Setup the audio codec device to start the audio playback or capture

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for codec driver instance.
device audio playback and capture selection.

simplesect.para: 0 if successful, negative errno code if failure.

briefdescription.para: Set codec to stop audio playback or capture.

detaileddescription.para: Setup the audio codec device to stop the audio playback or capture.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for codec driver instance.
device audio playback and capture selection.

simplesect.para: 0 if successful, negative errno code if failure.

briefdescription.para: write one block size data for audio playback.

detaileddescription.para: Write audio data to the device for audio playback without waiting for the data to be transmitted. This is a non-blocking API: the function returns as soon as the driver has accepted (or rejected) the buffer, not when the audio data has actually been played out.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the device structure for codec driver instance.
Pointer to data.
data size in bytes.

simplesect.para: 0 if successful, negative errno code if failure.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, audio_codec_tx_done_callback_t tx_cb, void *tx_cb_user_data, audio_codec_rx_done_callback_t rx_cb, void *rx_cb_user_data)
memberdef.name: audio_codec_register_done_callback

briefdescription.para: Register a callback function for codec one frame data tx/rx done.

detaileddescription.para:
The callback will be called from a DMA ISR (interrupt) context. Callbacks must therefore:
Be very short and avoid deep call stacks or large stack allocations, since the ISR stack is limited.
Do not sleep or block (do not wait on semaphores, mutexes, queues, etc.).
Only call APIs that are explicitly documented as IRQ/ISR-safe.
Avoid heavy processing or extensive logging; instead, defer such work to thread or workqueue context (for example, by submitting a work item).

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the audio codec device
The function that should be called when an DMA frame transmitted
The user data that will be passed to tx_cb
The function that should be called when an DMA frame received
The user data that will be passed to rx_cb

simplesect.para: 0 if successful, negative errno code if failure.
